test.details: 254 tests passed, 0 failures, 0 warnings, 0 skips in 71.7s

check_rust.details: 0 Rust tests (all Rust functionality tested via R integration tests)

appliedUpdates.description: Added 'generate_plots' command (.venv/bin/python3 benchmarks/generate_plots.py) for reproducible plot generation

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update analysis-worker skill's TDD step to clarify that for pure visualization/plot generation features, verification can be done by running the script and checking output file existence/sizes rather than writing a separate test file.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: generate-plots worker noted in skillFeedback that for visualization scripts, running the script and verifying outputs directly is more appropriate than a separate test file. The TDD pattern is better suited for R/Python analytical code than plot generation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update analysis-worker skill to mention that benchmark results in benchmarks/results/ may be gitignored, and workers should use 'git add -f' to force-add deliverable report files from those directories.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: write-report worker had to discover that benchmarks/results/ is in .gitignore and use 'git add -f benchmarks/results/report/report.md' to include the report in the commit. This pattern was not documented.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: 'When committing final deliverables (reports, documentation) that live in otherwise-gitignored results directories (e.g., benchmarks/results/report/), use git add -f to force-add them. This ensures deliverables are version-controlled even when raw intermediate data is excluded.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: write-report worker needed to force-add report.md from benchmarks/results/report/ which is inside the gitignored benchmarks/results/ directory. This pattern will recur for any future deliverables in results directories.
